Scenario: generatedScenario1
Completed At (UTC): 2026-03-03T05:53:03.1113484+00:00
Summary: Encounter complete.

Step 1
Student Choice: A. Start immediate cold-water immersion, remove equipment, assign one person to call EMS and one to monitor vitals.
Reasoning: cscvferfcw
Recommended: A. Start immediate cold-water immersion, remove equipment, assign one person to call EMS and one to monitor vitals.
Recommended Why: Correct. Cool first, transport second for exertional heat stroke.
Result: Correct. Cool first, transport second for exertional heat stroke.

Step 2
Student Choice: B. Stop immersion early and switch to passive cooling only.
Reasoning: rwfwe
Recommended: A. Continue immersion until under 39.0 C, provide oxygen, prepare IV access, and continue monitoring.
Recommended Why: Correct. Continue active cooling and supportive care.
Result: Suboptimal. Stopping early can lead to rebound hyperthermia.

Scenario: generatedScenario2
Completed At (UTC): 2026-03-03T05:53:38.8182682+00:00
Summary: Encounter complete.

Step 1
Student Choice: A. Give IM epinephrine immediately, call EMS, and position supine with legs elevated.
Reasoning: efcwefcw
Recommended: A. Give IM epinephrine immediately, call EMS, and position supine with legs elevated.
Recommended Why: Correct. Epinephrine is first-line and time critical.
Result: Correct. Epinephrine is first-line and time critical.

Step 2
Student Choice: B. Stop interventions and discharge to family because symptoms improved.
Reasoning: fewfw
Recommended: A. Prepare IV access, continue monitoring, and be ready for repeat IM epinephrine if symptoms persist.
Recommended Why: Correct. Continue active management and handoff planning.
Result: Unsafe. Biphasic or recurrent reactions can occur.

Scenario: liveDemoScenario

Summary: Encounter complete.

Step 1
Student Choice: C. Ask coach and teammates what happened before touching the athlete.
Reasoning: bjkbh
Recommended: A. Check responsiveness, breathing, and pulse immediately while directing your assistant to bring the AED and call EMS.
Recommended Why: Correct. Immediate primary assessment and early AED/EMS activation are critical.
Result: Incorrect. History is secondary to immediate ABC assessment in an unresponsive athlete.
